title: [v4.9-rhel] Update CI on v4.9-rhel to check for jira links
body:
This backports the changes from https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/23622 to ensure that PRs on the -rhel branches have a Jira references.

Also, we need to update DEST_BRANCH on this branch as that was not done yet.

Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/VROOM-20772

comments.body:
I intentionally made no reference to a Jira issue in this, and indeed, it failed with:
```
+ make test-jira-links-included  # /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/./contrib/cirrus/runner.sh:38 in _run_validate()
contrib/cirrus/pr-should-link-jira
pr-should-link-jira: PR does not include required references to Jira issues

Please add a reference to the related Jira ticket(s) by adding to the
description of the PR something like:

Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-50507

You can use multiple lines like this, but only one issue per line.

If your commit really, truly does not need a jira link, you can proceed
by asking a repo maintainer to set the 'No Jira Link' github label.
This will only be done when there's no reasonable alternative.
make: *** [Makefile:704: test-jira-links-included] Error 1
```
Lets see if I can make it pass by adding one.

Hahahahaha, well, I guess [it works!](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4859563134943232?logs=main#L82)
```
pr-should-link-jira: PR does not include required references to Jira issues

Please add a reference to the related Jira ticket(s) by adding to the
description of the PR something like:

Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-50507
```
I'll let someone else do the honors of testing the label and rerun. LGTM, although I don't understand `DEST_BRANCH` enough to be the one giving final approval here.
I added a reference to a jira (not a RHEL one though, but the RHIVOS one). How do I make it rerun the tests?

You repush. TL;DR Cirrus is a little counterintuitive in how it handles Github messages: no matter how many times you press Rerun, the value it sees for  `$CIRRUS_CHANGE_MESSAGE` is what it was at the time the push was made. It will never change. You need a new CI job. HTH.
Now with the jira link this passes. However, it fails some update test, which I don't really think can be related to this change, or am I missing something?

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
